title: Beatport Live Pool Party x Mood Child
description:
Hosted by Beatport Live in partnership with WMC, the Miami Music Week Hotel kicks off three days of poolside label showcases with Mood Child, Beatport Awards 2025 Hype Label of the Year.

Founded by Sirus Hood and Manda Moor, Mood Child is a global music and event brand built around emotion, instinct, and shared energy. The label brings together over 300 artists from across the electronic music spectrum and has hosted immersive events worldwide, from Ibiza takeovers to stages with elrow and Lovefest.

Label founders Manda Moor and Sirus Hood will lead the lineup, joined by DJ Sneak, Jean Pierre, and Ms. Mada for a very special evening.

Attendees can enjoy panoramic waterfront views, premium production, and world-class electronic music on one of Miami’s most iconic pool decks.

PLEASE NOTE: Cabana bookings are priced at $500 (for up to six guests) and are subject to an additional mandatory $1,000 minimum spend on food and beverage, exclusive of tax and service charge.

faqs.title: What are the dates and times for the pool party series?

You can gain access to all three parties plus access to Winter Music Conference by purchasing a WMC Pro Badge - more details at https://wintermusicconference.com/

faqs.title: Is this a ticketed event or can WMC badge holders attend for free?

faqs.title: Do tickets include access to all three days or is each day ticketed separately?

Purchasing one ticket via DICE will only enable entry for the day you have selected.

You can gain access to all three parties plus access to Winter Music Conference by purchasing a WMC Pro Badge - more details at https://wintermusicconference.com/

faqs.title: Can I change the date of my ticket? Are refunds allowed?

faqs.title: Are swimwear and pool access allowed?

faqs.title: Will food and drinks be available for purchase?

faqs.title: Are outside food or drinks allowed?

faqs.title: Is there a bag policy?

title: Miami Music Week | 20Five8Records x Groovemates
description:
The night rises above Wynwood at Astra Rooftop, located at 2121 NW 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33127.

Inspired by the goddess of the stars, Astra is Miami’s premier open-air rooftop destination, where Mediterranean elegance meets the electric pulse of Miami Music Week. With sweeping skyline views, handcrafted cocktails, and elevated Mediterranean cuisine, Astra sets the stage for unforgettable moments under the Miami sky.

For Miami Music Week 2026, a full week of world-class talent takes over the rooftop as the most celebrated artists in dance music guide us on a sonic journey high above the city, offering what many consider the best view of the Miami skyline. Each night brings a refined yet high-energy experience, blending global headliners and rising selectors into a seamless progression of sound

From deep and hypnotic grooves to peak-hour intensity, every set is intentional, building momentum and holding the rooftop from start to finish. As the skyline glows and the terrace comes alive, the rhythm unites the crowd beneath the stars. Astra is not just a party, it is where music, atmosphere, and Miami nightlife meet at full elevation.

Guests are encouraged to bring positive energy and respect for the space and one another.

21+ only. All ticket sales are final.
location: 2121 Northwest 2nd Avenue, Miami, Florida 33127, United States

title: Satoshi Tomiie, Melody & More (Nomad Talent MMW Opener)
description:
Tuesday, March 24, Nomad brings its roster to the forefront with a night that feels more like a private summit than a party. Before the city spins into overdrive, this is where artists, agents, and heads connect over serious music.

Satoshi Tomiie leads the charge. A true pioneer whose legacy runs from “Tears” with Frankie Knuckles to his forward-thinking Abstract Architecture imprint, Satoshi bridges house and techno with the depth of a jazz-trained musician and the precision of a hardware purist. Decades in, he’s still pushing the conversation forward.

Melody represents the RA+RE and Ninkasi ethos with punch and personality. From Berlin’s Club der Visionaere and Hoppetosse to Rex Club in Paris, her sets cut through with electro, breakbeat, and 90s-rooted house and techno pulled from a true digger’s collection.

Ohm Hourani brings Montreal’s experimental edge. Founder of ANOMA and a live performer shaped by jazz and modular exploration, his sound blends organic recordings with intricate engineering. Expect something thoughtful, minimal, and subtly wild.

Kurilo, Ukrainian-born and now NYC-based, rounds out the bill with a vinyl-driven approach rooted in percussion and 90s energy. With over 25 releases and appearances from Europe to Boiler Room Berlin, he delivers raw, focused grooves with real depth.

This is the opening note of the week.

No chaos yet. Just the right room, the right people, and the right sound.
location: 2 S Miami Ave, Miami, Florida 33130, United States

faqs.title: Dress code?
faqs.body: We hope you come dressed to express your truest self. However, for the safety of you and other Space Invaders, footwear that lacks a back strap is not allowed. faqs.title: What items are allowed?

In addition, we don’t allow weapons of any kind, including firearms, knives and pepper spray. This is in the best interest of all Space Invaders and helps us all create a positive and safe environment inside.

title: Beatport Live Pool Party x 20 Years Rekids
description:
Hosted by Beatport Live in partnership with WMC, the Miami Music Week Hotel presents day two of three poolside label showcases with Rekids, entering its 20th year in 2026.

In two decades of operations, Matt Edwards, aka Radio Slave, has helmed one of the most impactful dance music labels of the 21st century. Storied artists like Robert Hood and Ian Pooley have called the label their home, while once fledgling acts like Nina Kraviz and Peggy Gou cut their teeth with Rekids early in their careers. Never ploughing just one furrow, deep house, house, techno, electro, ambient works, and more have flowed out of Edwards' imprint, with his own critically acclaimed material traversing each genre touchpoint too.

With a label residency at Berghain that stretches almost 19 years and the House of Rekids club nights, the imprint still remains fiercely independent, punching above its weight and still focused on the core message: it’s the music that matters.

Label head Radio Slave will be joined by Danny Tenaglia, DJ Minx, Tal Fussman, Doc Martin, Anja Schneider, and William Kiss, with a series of exclusive back-to-back sets!

Attendees can enjoy panoramic waterfront views, premium production, and world-class electronic music on one of Miami’s most iconic pool decks.

PLEASE NOTE: Cabana bookings are priced at $500 (for up to six guests) and are subject to an additional mandatory $1,000 minimum spend on food and beverage, exclusive of tax and service charge.
location: 270 Biscayne Boulevard, Miami, Florida 33131, United States

title: Balance Miami
description:
We are proud to present Balance Miami during the 2026 Winter Music Conference & Miami Music Week, as part of the 'Road to Balance Croatia’ event series, a global festival journey for progressive and melodic house music lovers, connecting sound and culture across music communities, culminating in the Balance Croatia Festival as its creative heart, which takes place August 6th-10th 2026 at The Garden Resort in Tisno.

Through a curated series of international events, the Balance Croatia festival unites leading and emerging artists and forward-thinking audiences to celebrate electronic music, balance, sustainability, and meaningful connection across continents.

From intimate showcases to larger-scale events, the series creates a bridge between international scenes and local communities. Events in Zagreb and Osijek will spotlight Croatia’s growing presence on the global electronic music map, while London, Glasgow, Rio, Los Angeles, Melbourne, Sydney, and Buenos Aires will also serve as key international touch-points, introducing the Balance Croatia Festival ethos to audiences worldwide.

The first 'Road to Balance Croatia' event for 2026 will launch in Miami on Wednesday, March 25th at 1-800 Lucky in Wynwood during the Winter Music Conference and Miami Music Week where industry and electronic music lovers will gather to celebrate a week of world class artists and events.

Balance Miami will showcase the talents of Guy J, Guy Mantzur, Ezequiel Arias, Niki Sadeki, and Miami local Lucas Zarate, for a day and night of unforgettable music at one of the the hippest indoor/outdoor spaces in Miami.
location: 143 Northwest 23rd Street, Miami, Florida 33127, United States

title: That Thing on Wednesday - HoH x ENJOY x Vice City
description:
That Thing On Wednesday

House of Huemans x Enjoy x Vice City

The one you’ll be talking about on Thursday.

Miami Music Week is loud. Everyone’s got a lineup, a rooftop, a “don’t miss this.” So let us be straight with you — That Thing On Wednesday isn’t competing with the spectacle. It’s the antidote to it.

While the rest of the week fights for your attention with headliners and hype, we’re bringing something rarer: a room that actually breathes. A sound that moves because it means something. A crowd that came to feel it, not just post it.

AbueKev · Arht · Carlos Diaz · Chillin Williams · Hotboxx · Mokam · Sundoll · Tegi · Vampire Sex

∙ more special guests

Nine artists. One direction. All the way in.

Presented by three of Miami’s most trusted names in underground culture — House of Huemans, Enjoy, and Vice City — this is the Wednesday night that reminds you why you came to Miami in the first place.

No ego. Just music. Show up early. Stay late.

Part of the official Miami Music Week/Winter Music Conference 2026 program.

Grab your ticket before it’s That Thing You Missed On Wednesday.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

title: The Hideout Presents Something Light Showcase
description:
The night rises above Wynwood at Astra Rooftop, located at 2121 NW 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33127.

Inspired by the goddess of the stars, Astra is Miami’s premier open-air rooftop destination, where Mediterranean elegance meets the electric pulse of Miami Music Week. With sweeping skyline views, handcrafted cocktails, and elevated Mediterranean cuisine, Astra sets the stage for unforgettable moments under the Miami sky.

For Miami Music Week 2026, a full week of world-class talent takes over the rooftop as the most celebrated artists in dance music guide us on a sonic journey high above the city, offering what many consider the best view of the Miami skyline. Each night brings a refined yet high-energy experience, blending global headliners and rising selectors into a seamless progression of sound

From deep and hypnotic grooves to peak-hour intensity, every set is intentional, building momentum and holding the rooftop from start to finish. As the skyline glows and the terrace comes alive, the rhythm unites the crowd beneath the stars. Astra is not just a party, it is where music, atmosphere, and Miami nightlife meet at full elevation.

Guests are encouraged to bring positive energy and respect for the space and one another.

21+ only. All ticket sales are final.

title: Massano
description:
**MASSANO**

is a British DJ and producer from Liverpool who has quickly become one of the leading names in melodic techno and house. Known for his dark, cinematic sound design, driving basslines, and emotionally charged melodies, his productions blend powerful grooves with atmospheric tension, making them staples on major festival stages and underground dance floors alike. He broke through with “The Feeling” and his Afterlife EP In My System, which topped Beatport charts and gained heavy support from Tale Of Us and other global tastemakers. With releases on Afterlife and his own label Simulate, plus performances at major international festivals and headline shows worldwide, Massano has established himself as one of the most impactful rising artists in modern melodic techno.

faqs.title: When do events end?
faqs.body: Closing time varies per event. Every event may end later or before the given end date and time on your DICE ticket.

faqs.title: Acceptable forms of identification?
faqs.body: You must present a valid ID at the door in order to enter. We only accept US Government issued ID cards. If you are visiting from another country you MUST present a valid passport with up to date photos. International ID Cards are only accepted if a clear picture of your passport is presented. Access is still up to the doorman's discretion with NO REFUND if access is denied.

faqs.title: Entry is at the discretion of door staff.
faqs.body: \*Entrance inside is ultimately at the discretion of the door staff. If you exhibit behavior at the door which is deemed unsafe to others or will generally lower the quality of the experience for others you will be denied entry. NO REFUNDS will be available.

faqs.title: Dress code?
faqs.body: We hope you come dressed to express your truest self. However, for the safety of you and other Space Invaders, footwear that lacks a back strap is not allowed. This includes items such as sandals and heels with no back strap. Gym shorts and other pieces of athletic wear are also not permitted.

faqs.title: What items are allowed?
faqs.body: Purses and other bags are allowed, but there are many small or large items that cannot be taken inside with you. These include hoola hoops, glow sticks and anything else that can be used as a projectile and cause accidental harm. In addition, we don’t allow weapons of any kind, including firearms, knives and pepper spray. This is in the best interest of all Space Invaders and helps us all create a positive and safe environment inside.

faqs.title: Club Space is cigarette free.
faqs.body: Club Space is now cigarette free. We thank you for not smoking cigarettes at Club Space.

title: Beatport Live Pool Party: Hot Creations x TSZR
description:
Hosted by Beatport Live in partnership with WMC, the Miami Music Week Hotel presents day three of our poolside showcases with a special label B2B: Hot Creations vs Three Six Zero Recordings.

Since 2010, Hot Creations has been a definitive force in house music, with founders Jamie Jones and Lee Foss pioneering a soulful, melodic sound that has shaped dancefloors globally. This legacy finds a perfect counterpart in Three Six Zero Recordings, a forward-thinking independent label built around creative freedom. Since 2014, they have acted as a platform for boundary-pushing artists such as Dom Dolla, Goldie, WILLOW, creating music that resonates from the underground to global arenas. The two imprints share deep creative ties and a natural synergy, making this showcase a rare moment where their distinct but complementary visions collide.

A very special lineup featuring a collaborative curation of core artists from both rosters will be announced soon, marking a one-of-a-kind summit of two electronic powerhouses. Attendees can enjoy panoramic waterfront views, premium production, and world-class electronic music on one of Miami’s most iconic pool decks.

PLEASE NOTE: Cabana bookings are priced at $500 (for up to six guests) and are subject to an additional mandatory $1,000 minimum spend on food and beverage, exclusive of tax and service charge.

title: Rvdiovctive x Groove Factory x Clique Cabana
description:
RVDIOVCTIVE, Groove Factory, and Clique Cabana unite for a Miami Music Week session built on pure dancefloor chemistry. A collision of crews known for turning fleeting moments into unforgettable memories. Born from Brooklyn warehouses and carried to dancefloors around the world, RVDIOVCTIVE meets Groove Factory’s driving, groove-forward West Coast ethos and Clique Cabana’s effortlessly soulful Atlanta touch. Three crews united by a deep commitment to quality music and the communities that grow around it.

Soul Clap set the tone with their signature cosmic funk, while Nala and Demi Riquísimo collide for a rare B2B. Her raw, magnetic edge meeting his radiant, psychedelic-leaning euphoria in a set built for full surrender. Life on Planets joins Devon James, where their shared feel-good sensibility takes hold. Uplifting, transportive, and deeply rooted in the kind of emotional connection that defines the best dancefloors.

With support from the RVDIOVCTIVE, Groove Factory, Clique Cabana, and Ritmo families, the energy flows from the very first record. Happy hour and free entry from 4–8PM set the pace, with Coyo Taco serving food and drinks to keep you moving from day into night. This is Miami Music Week as it’s meant to be…timeless grooves, open hearts, and a room full of beautiful souls you won’t want to leave. 🌴

title: UNION SHOWCASE - PEPPE CITARELLA & FRIENDS
description:
The night rises above Wynwood at Astra Rooftop, located at 2121 NW 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33127.

Inspired by the goddess of the stars, Astra is Miami’s premier open-air rooftop destination, where Mediterranean elegance meets the electric pulse of Miami Music Week. With sweeping skyline views, handcrafted cocktails, and elevated Mediterranean cuisine, Astra sets the stage for unforgettable moments under the Miami sky.

For Miami Music Week 2026, a full week of world-class talent takes over the rooftop as the most celebrated artists in dance music guide us on a sonic journey high above the city, offering what many consider the best view of the Miami skyline. Each night brings a refined yet high-energy experience, blending global headliners and rising selectors into a seamless progression of sound

From deep and hypnotic grooves to peak-hour intensity, every set is intentional, building momentum and holding the rooftop from start to finish. As the skyline glows and the terrace comes alive, the rhythm unites the crowd beneath the stars. Astra is not just a party, it is where music, atmosphere, and Miami nightlife meet at full elevation.

Guests are encouraged to bring positive energy and respect for the space and one another.

21+ only. All ticket sales are final.

title: Nervous X Negroni
description:
Nervous Records is currently America's Longest Standing Dance Brand. Having started back in 1991 with releases from Louie Vega, Todd Terry, Kenny Dope and Roger Sanchez, the label has continued throughout its 35 year existence to be a source of many of the world's most memorable dancefloor bombs. Just recently Nervous released worldwide anthems from Mau P ("The Less I Know The Better), Mochakk ("Da Fonk Feat. Joni") and Loofy ("Last Night.) In addition to the music, Nervous Records has a highly innovative merchandise line that highlights its universally recognized Cartoon Logo, and an active Event Promotion Team that produces and curates some of the most memorable DJ events in its homebase of New York as well as in Miami.

Nervous Records has always had a strong presence at Miami Music Week, and 2026 sees Nervous makes its debut at the very popular and fashionable Negroni.

Fire Line-up to be announced!

title: The Disco Express: MMW Label Showcase x We Group Hi-Fi
description:
**The Disco Express** lands in Miami for a special label showcase as part of **Miami Music Week.**

Born in London and now spreading soul fuel worldwide, The Disco Express brings its signature blend of house, disco and funk to Miami Sound with a heavyweight lineup of family and friends.

House music royalty **Barbara Tucker** joins us for a very special guest performance, alongside NYC’s rising force branqueeno representing Sisters In Sound. Label founder Bustin' Loose leads the charge, joined by TDE's NY favourites Roland & Brother Rich.

Expect uplifting vocals, rolling bass lines and peak-time heaters from our label til the early hours.

Full steam ahead. Miami, are you ready?

This event is party of the **We Group Hi-Fi series**, in which festival and event production collective We Group takes over Miami Sound Bar during **Miami Music Week** - showcasing iconic labels that bring a diverse line up of talent to Downtown Miami's favourite spot for hi-fi sound.

faqs.title: When do events end?
faqs.body: Closing time varies per event. Every event may end later or before the given end date and time on your DICE ticket.

faqs.title: Acceptable forms of identification?
faqs.body: You must present a valid ID at the door in order to enter. We only accept US Government issued ID cards. If you are visiting from another country you MUST present a valid passport with up to date photos. International ID Cards are only accepted if a clear picture of your passport is presented. Access is still up to the doorman's discretion with NO REFUND if access is denied.

faqs.title: Entry is at the discretion of door staff.
faqs.body: \*Entrance inside is ultimately at the discretion of the door staff. If you exhibit behavior at the door which is deemed unsafe to others or will generally lower the quality of the experience for others you will be denied entry. NO REFUNDS will be available.

faqs.title: Dress code?
faqs.body: We hope you come dressed to express your truest self. However, for the safety of you and other Space Invaders, footwear that lacks a back strap is not allowed. This includes items such as sandals and heels with no back strap. Gym shorts and other pieces of athletic wear are also not permitted.

faqs.title: What items are allowed?
faqs.body: Purses and other bags are allowed, but there are many small or large items that cannot be taken inside with you. These include hoola hoops, glow sticks and anything else that can be used as a projectile and cause accidental harm. In addition, we don’t allow weapons of any kind, including firearms, knives and pepper spray. This is in the best interest of all Space Invaders and helps us all create a positive and safe environment inside.

faqs.title: Club Space is cigarette free.
faqs.body: Club Space is now cigarette free. We thank you for not smoking cigarettes at Club Space.

title: WITTY TUNES SHOWCASE - EDD & FRIENDS
description:
The night rises above Wynwood at Astra Rooftop, located at 2121 NW 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33127.

Inspired by the goddess of the stars, Astra is Miami’s premier open-air rooftop destination, where Mediterranean elegance meets the electric pulse of Miami Music Week. With sweeping skyline views, handcrafted cocktails, and elevated Mediterranean cuisine, Astra sets the stage for unforgettable moments under the Miami sky.

For Miami Music Week 2026, a full week of world-class talent takes over the rooftop as the most celebrated artists in dance music guide us on a sonic journey high above the city, offering what many consider the best view of the Miami skyline. Each night brings a refined yet high-energy experience, blending global headliners and rising selectors into a seamless progression of sound

From deep and hypnotic grooves to peak-hour intensity, every set is intentional, building momentum and holding the rooftop from start to finish. As the skyline glows and the terrace comes alive, the rhythm unites the crowd beneath the stars. Astra is not just a party, it is where music, atmosphere, and Miami nightlife meet at full elevation.

Guests are encouraged to bring positive energy and respect for the space and one another.

21+ only. All ticket sales are final.

title: Ferry Corsten + Giuseppe Ottaviani + Billy Gillies + Amy Wiles + Luccio
description:
\*\*FERRY CORSTEN  
\*\*Dutch electronic music icon and trance pioneer with a career spanning over two decades. Known for classics like “Punk” and “Radio Crash,” he’s also the creative force behind aliases such as System F and Gouryella, shaping the global trance and progressive scene.

\*\*GIUSEPPE OTTAVIANI  
\*\*Italian trance powerhouse known for his high-energy, emotionally charged sound and masterful live performances. A former member of Nu NRG, he’s celebrated for pushing trance forward through his Live 3.0 and Live 4.0 shows, blending classic trance emotion with cutting-edge production.

BILLY GILLIES  
Northern Irish DJ and electronic music producer known for his vibrant and emotive style rooted in trance and progressive dance music. Hailing from Belfast, he has become one of the most exciting rising stars in the global electronic scene.

\*\*AMY WILES  
\*\*British DJ and electronic music producer known for her melodic trance and progressive dance sound that blends lush emotional melodies with high-energy rhythms — a style that resonates both on large festival stages and in club settings.

\*\*LUCCIO  
His music blends trance energy with progressive, creating sets and tracks that resonate with both club and festival crowds. Over the years, Luccio has played at major U.S. festivals and venues including UMF, EDC, Okeechobee Festival, and Groove Cruise, and has held residencies at iconic Miami clubs like Club Space

ALL SALES ARE FINAL\*\*
location: 2250 Northwest 2nd Avenue, Miami, Florida 33125, United States

faqs.title: ID’s, Entry, Re-entry, Last Entry
faqs.body:
**No physical ID = No Entry**. This is a legal requirement. We only accept valid physical government-issued photo ID’s. We do not accept photos of ID’s. **All ID’s must be physically scanned.**  
**Re-entry:** You may leave and return. Depending on your admission type, you may have to pay to re-enter. Check with the door staff before leaving. You must go through all security checks when you return. Your re-entry can be refused at the discretion of the door person, if you are too intoxicated, for example.  
**Last Entry** is 15 minutes before closing or event end.  
Admission is at the discretion of the door staff. There are no refunds if entry is denied.

faqs.title: Safety Checks, Searches, Re-Entry, and Last Entry
faqs.body:
Absolutely everyone must be searched before entering our space. No weapons of any kind are permitted. Out side drinks and alcohol are not permitted. Drugs are not permitted.

**Prohibited Bags:** Backpacks, duffel bags, and any bag large enough to carry a change of clothes are not allowed inside the venue.

**Allowed bags:** Fanny packs, purses, and other small bags used to carry essential personal items (wallet, phone, lipstick, etc.) are generally permitted with the reserved right to refuse any bag for any reason at the discretion of Eagle staff without explanation.

All bags will be searched upon entry and exit, at the discretion of staff.

faqs.title: Cell Phone Checks
faqs.body:
We prevent pickpockets from stealing your phone by requesting at all exits that you unlock any cell phone that you are carrying in the presence of the security staff person. You will also be checked with a metal detector to make sure all cell phones are unlocked.

If you cannot or refuse to unlock a phone you are carrying, you will be “asked politely” to wait with several of our security guards until the police arrive. We are not asking to look in your phone or even at whatever is on the home screen. We merely need to verify that you are able to unlock the phone. This measure is to prevent pick pockets from stealing your phone.

If you refuse to open your phone or we call the Police, you will be permanently banned from the Eagle.

faqs.title: Skip the Line
faqs.body:
Skip the Line tickets allow you to bypass general admission lines at the entrance up to the point of security screening.

All entries, including Skip the Line, must pass through security screening.

Skip the Line access applies only to the ticket holder. Guests without Skip the Line tickets may not accompany you through this lane.

faqs.title: Dress Code
faqs.body:
We celebrate self-expression and diversity.

The dress code is “be street legal” except at certain times in the space we call CODE Bar.

Drink Specials on nights like Jockstrap Night or Underwear Night may be available only to those in the prescribed attire of the night.

Open toed or backless shoes like sandals and flip flops are not allowed in the CODE Bar. See more here about rules specific to the CODE Bar

Gender Expression is welcome. Be yourself.

faqs.title: When do events end?
faqs.body: Closing time varies per event. Every event may end later or before the given end date and time on your DICE ticket.

faqs.title: Acceptable forms of identification?
faqs.body: You must present a valid ID at the door in order to enter. We only accept US Government issued ID cards. If you are visiting from another country you MUST present a valid passport with up to date photos. International ID Cards are only accepted if a clear picture of your passport is presented. Access is still up to the doorman's discretion with NO REFUND if access is denied.

faqs.title: Entry is at the discretion of door staff.
faqs.body: \*Entrance inside is ultimately at the discretion of the door staff. If you exhibit behavior at the door which is deemed unsafe to others or will generally lower the quality of the experience for others you will be denied entry. NO REFUNDS will be available.

faqs.title: Dress code?
faqs.body: We hope you come dressed to express your truest self. However, for the safety of you and other Space Invaders, footwear that lacks a back strap is not allowed. This includes items such as sandals and heels with no back strap. Gym shorts and other pieces of athletic wear are also not permitted.

faqs.title: What items are allowed?
faqs.body: Purses and other bags are allowed, but there are many small or large items that cannot be taken inside with you. These include hoola hoops, glow sticks and anything else that can be used as a projectile and cause accidental harm. In addition, we don’t allow weapons of any kind, including firearms, knives and pepper spray. This is in the best interest of all Space Invaders and helps us all create a positive and safe environment inside.

title: Derrick Does Disco — Deep House Brunch | Essential Miami MMW
description:
Ten years. 44+ headliners. One rule: the music comes first.

Deep House Brunch is the deep house institution that Miami Music Week heads have been whispering about since 2014. No LED walls. No influencer tables. Just a rooftop, a sound system, and a lineup that speaks for itself.

This year? Derrick Carter does disco.

The Chicago legend brings his crate-deep record collection to BOHO House for an open-air sunset session — think disco edits bleeding into deep house while the Miami skyline turns gold behind you.

Supporting: Jay-J (Grammy-nominated, Shifted Music), Scotty Boy, Mark Brickman, and Saliva Commandos.

Plus live vocal performances by Big Broojlyn Red and Nicole Graham - the thing that makes ESSENTIAL different from everything else during Music Week.

Sun. Sky. Sound. See you on the Dancefloor.

promoter: Presented by Deep House Brunch | Essential

title: Louie Vega & Friends: Moodymann, Anané, Karizma
description:
Louie Vega’s Miami Music Week parties have taken on legendary status through the years, as his special guests include some of greatest performers in the world of dance music and beyond. For the 2026 edition of LOUIE VEGA & Friends during MMW, “The Maestro” has brought together a spectacular group of DJ / Producers representing the best of now and future generations. On board for this very special Saturday outdoor day-into-night event are MOODYMANN, ANANÉ and KARIZMA. House Vibes like nowhere else from start to finish!

This event is 21+

Table Reservations can be made by purchasing one of the VIP TABLES listed on Tablelist.com. Please note that table availability is limited.

All ticket sales are final. All ticket prices include sales tax + platform fees

Ride shares are encouraged. Limited, free public parking is available across from ZeyZey’s main entrance (61st Street and 4th Ave).

Please be advised that photos and videos may be taken at this event for ZeyZey marketing purposes. By attending, you consent to your image and likeness being used across our platforms.

Events at ZeyZey may include flashing lights and strobe effects, which may affect photosensitive individuals.

ZeyZey Miami: An open-air cultural venue and community gathering space, offering a dynamic lineup of experiences—from laid-back, family-friendly afternoons to high-energy nights soundtracked by live music from around the world.

title: Black Coffee, Carlita & Kaz James at the Racetrack: MMW 26
description:
Black Coffee with Carlita & Kaz James in Open Air at the Racetrack at Hialeah Park Casino for MMW, Saturday March 28th.

6PM - 1AM

GA 18+ | VIP 21+
location: 2200 East 4th Avenue, Hialeah, Florida 33013, United States

faqs.title: Venue entrance?
faqs.body:
SE Entrance

2200 E 4th Ave, Hialeah, FL 33013

faqs.title: Dress code?
faqs.body: We hope you come dressed to express your truest self. However, for the safety of you and other Space Invaders, footwear that lacks a back strap is not allowed. This includes items such as sandals and heels with no back strap. Gym shorts and other pieces of athletic wear are also not permitted.

faqs.title: Event hours?
faqs.body:
Event hours are at 6PM to 1AM.

Doors at 6PM

faqs.title: Acceptable forms of identification (ID)?
faqs.body: You must present a valid ID at the door in order to enter. We only accept US Government issued ID cards. If you are visiting from another country you MUST present a valid passport with up to date photos. International ID Cards are only accepted if a clear picture of your passport is presented. Access is still up to the doorman's discretion with NO REFUND if access is denied.

faqs.title: Entry is at the discretion of door staff.
faqs.body: \*Entrance inside is ultimately at the discretion of the door staff. If you exhibit behavior at the door which is deemed unsafe to others or will generally lower the quality of the experience for others you will be denied entry. NO REFUNDS will be available.

faqs.title: Purse & bag policy.
faqs.body: \*Purses and other bags are allowed, but there are many small or large items that cannot be taken inside with you. Bags are subject to search. These include hoola hoops, glow sticks and anything else that can be used as a projectile and cause accidental harm. In addition, we don’t allow weapons of any kind, including firearms, knives and pepper spray. This is in the best interest of all Space Invaders and helps us all create a positive and safe environment inside.

title: FREEZE PROJECT & INHOUSE RECORDS
description:
The night rises above Wynwood at Astra Rooftop, located at 2121 NW 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33127.

Inspired by the goddess of the stars, Astra is Miami’s premier open-air rooftop destination, where Mediterranean elegance meets the electric pulse of Miami Music Week. With sweeping skyline views, handcrafted cocktails, and elevated Mediterranean cuisine, Astra sets the stage for unforgettable moments under the Miami sky.

For Miami Music Week 2026, a full week of world-class talent takes over the rooftop as the most celebrated artists in dance music guide us on a sonic journey high above the city, offering what many consider the best view of the Miami skyline. Each night brings a refined yet high-energy experience, blending global headliners and rising selectors into a seamless progression of sound

From deep and hypnotic grooves to peak-hour intensity, every set is intentional, building momentum and holding the rooftop from start to finish. As the skyline glows and the terrace comes alive, the rhythm unites the crowd beneath the stars. Astra is not just a party, it is where music, atmosphere, and Miami nightlife meet at full elevation.

Guests are encouraged to bring positive energy and respect for the space and one another.

21+ only. All ticket sales are final.
location: 2121 Northwest 2nd Avenue, Miami, Florida 33127, United States

venues.address: 2121 Northwest 2nd Avenue, Miami, Florida 33127, United States

faqs.title: ID’s, Entry, Re-entry, Last Entry
faqs.body:
**No physical ID = No Entry**. This is a legal requirement. We only accept valid physical government-issued photo ID’s. We do not accept photos of ID’s. **All ID’s must be physically scanned.**  
**Re-entry:** You may leave and return. Depending on your admission type, you may have to pay to re-enter. Check with the door staff before leaving. You must go through all security checks when you return. Your re-entry can be refused at the discretion of the door person, if you are too intoxicated, for example.  
**Last Entry** is 15 minutes before closing or event end.  
Admission is at the discretion of the door staff. There are no refunds if entry is denied.

faqs.title: Safety Checks, Searches, Re-Entry, and Last Entry
faqs.body:
Absolutely everyone must be searched before entering our space. No weapons of any kind are permitted. Out side drinks and alcohol are not permitted. Drugs are not permitted.

**Prohibited Bags:** Backpacks, duffel bags, and any bag large enough to carry a change of clothes are not allowed inside the venue.

**Allowed bags:** Fanny packs, purses, and other small bags used to carry essential personal items (wallet, phone, lipstick, etc.) are generally permitted with the reserved right to refuse any bag for any reason at the discretion of Eagle staff without explanation.

All bags will be searched upon entry and exit, at the discretion of staff.

faqs.title: Cell Phone Checks
faqs.body:
We prevent pickpockets from stealing your phone by requesting at all exits that you unlock any cell phone that you are carrying in the presence of the security staff person. You will also be checked with a metal detector to make sure all cell phones are unlocked.

If you cannot or refuse to unlock a phone you are carrying, you will be “asked politely” to wait with several of our security guards until the police arrive. We are not asking to look in your phone or even at whatever is on the home screen. We merely need to verify that you are able to unlock the phone. This measure is to prevent pick pockets from stealing your phone.

If you refuse to open your phone or we call the Police, you will be permanently banned from the Eagle.

faqs.title: Skip the Line
faqs.body:
Skip the Line tickets allow you to bypass general admission lines at the entrance up to the point of security screening.

All entries, including Skip the Line, must pass through security screening.

Skip the Line access applies only to the ticket holder. Guests without Skip the Line tickets may not accompany you through this lane.

faqs.title: Dress Code
faqs.body:
We celebrate self-expression and diversity.

The dress code is “be street legal” except at certain times in the space we call CODE Bar.

Drink Specials on nights like Jockstrap Night or Underwear Night may be available only to those in the prescribed attire of the night.

Open toed or backless shoes like sandals and flip flops are not allowed in the CODE Bar. See more here about rules specific to the CODE Bar

Gender Expression is welcome. Be yourself.

title: Pendulum by Steve Lawler (MMW 2026)
description:
Miami Music Week welcomes the return of Pendulum, Steve Lawler’s globally respected project rooted in quality, balance, and deeper musical exploration.

For this special MMW edition at MODE, Steve Lawler brings the signature Pendulum sound to Downtown Miami. Expect a journey through deeper shades of house, layered percussion, tribal rhythms, and hypnotic grooves that connect movement with atmosphere. Pendulum is about equilibrium on the dancefloor, where energy builds naturally and the music speaks with intention.

Joining Lawler is Eli Fola performing a Hybrid Live set, blending electronic production with live instrumentation and Afro-inspired rhythms for a truly immersive experience. Also on the lineup is Ella Romand with her Hybrid Live performance, bringing textured, melodic depth and organic elements that align seamlessly with the Pendulum ethos.

This is not a typical peak-time sprint. It is a carefully curated progression designed for those who understand the power of space, rhythm, and connection.

Pendulum. Miami Music Week. One night of balance at MODE.

faqs.title: When do events end?
faqs.body: Closing time varies per event. Every event may end later or before the given end date and time on your DICE ticket.

faqs.title: Acceptable forms of identification?
faqs.body: You must present a valid ID at the door in order to enter. We only accept US Government issued ID cards. If you are visiting from another country you MUST present a valid passport with up to date photos. International ID Cards are only accepted if a clear picture of your passport is presented. Access is still up to the doorman's discretion with NO REFUND if access is denied.

faqs.title: Entry is at the discretion of door staff.
faqs.body: \*Entrance inside is ultimately at the discretion of the door staff. If you exhibit behavior at the door which is deemed unsafe to others or will generally lower the quality of the experience for others you will be denied entry. NO REFUNDS will be available.

faqs.title: Dress code?
faqs.body: We hope you come dressed to express your truest self. However, for the safety of you and other Space Invaders, footwear that lacks a back strap is not allowed. This includes items such as sandals and heels with no back strap. Gym shorts and other pieces of athletic wear are also not permitted.

faqs.title: What items are allowed?
faqs.body: Purses and other bags are allowed, but there are many small or large items that cannot be taken inside with you. These include hoola hoops, glow sticks and anything else that can be used as a projectile and cause accidental harm. In addition, we don’t allow weapons of any kind, including firearms, knives and pepper spray. This is in the best interest of all Space Invaders and helps us all create a positive and safe environment inside.

faqs.title: Club Space is cigarette free.
faqs.body: Club Space is now cigarette free. We thank you for not smoking cigarettes at Club Space.

title: Miami Music Week Closing Party | LIBERO
description:
The night rises above Wynwood at Astra Rooftop, located at 2121 NW 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33127.

Inspired by the goddess of the stars, Astra is Miami’s premier open-air rooftop destination, where Mediterranean elegance meets the electric pulse of Miami Music Week. With sweeping skyline views, handcrafted cocktails, and elevated Mediterranean cuisine, Astra sets the stage for unforgettable moments under the Miami sky.

For Miami Music Week 2026, a full week of world-class talent takes over the rooftop as the most celebrated artists in dance music guide us on a sonic journey high above the city, offering what many consider the best view of the Miami skyline. Each night brings a refined yet high-energy experience, blending global headliners and rising selectors into a seamless progression of sound

From deep and hypnotic grooves to peak-hour intensity, every set is intentional, building momentum and holding the rooftop from start to finish. As the skyline glows and the terrace comes alive, the rhythm unites the crowd beneath the stars. Astra is not just a party, it is where music, atmosphere, and Miami nightlife meet at full elevation.

Guests are encouraged to bring positive energy and respect for the space and one another.

21+ only. All ticket sales are final.

come as you are -- just safely!

title: Deeperfect Showcase (MMW 2026)
description:
Deeperfect by Stefano Noferini takes over MODE this Miami Music Week for a Sunday built for true house heads.

Stefano brings his globally respected imprint to Downtown Miami for a full-label showcase that’s all groove, no filler. Expect driving basslines, rolling percussion, and that unmistakable Deeperfect energy that’s shaped dance floors around the world.

Leading the charge is Stefano Noferini, joined by a stacked A–Z lineup featuring Dimmish, Joe Vanditti, Mandiz, and Slugg. From deep warm-up moments to peak-time pressure, this one is designed to move start to finish.

Lock in your plans early. Music Week Sundays don’t play.

faqs.title: When do events end?
faqs.body: Closing time varies per event. Every event may end later or before the given end date and time on your DICE ticket.

faqs.title: Acceptable forms of identification?
faqs.body: You must present a valid ID at the door in order to enter. We only accept US Government issued ID cards. If you are visiting from another country you MUST present a valid passport with up to date photos. International ID Cards are only accepted if a clear picture of your passport is presented. Access is still up to the doorman's discretion with NO REFUND if access is denied.

faqs.title: Entry is at the discretion of door staff.
faqs.body: \*Entrance inside is ultimately at the discretion of the door staff. If you exhibit behavior at the door which is deemed unsafe to others or will generally lower the quality of the experience for others you will be denied entry. NO REFUNDS will be available.

faqs.title: Dress code?
faqs.body: We hope you come dressed to express your truest self. However, for the safety of you and other Space Invaders, footwear that lacks a back strap is not allowed. This includes items such as sandals and heels with no back strap. Gym shorts and other pieces of athletic wear are also not permitted.

faqs.title: What items are allowed?
faqs.body: Purses and other bags are allowed, but there are many small or large items that cannot be taken inside with you. These include hoola hoops, glow sticks and anything else that can be used as a projectile and cause accidental harm. In addition, we don’t allow weapons of any kind, including firearms, knives and pepper spray. This is in the best interest of all Space Invaders and helps us all create a positive and safe environment inside.

faqs.title: Club Space is cigarette free.
faqs.body: Club Space is now cigarette free. We thank you for not smoking cigarettes at Club Space.

title: ATC: CHESSTER, ANDY LOTTI, MADISON PARK..
description: After 3:00 am every day of the week, the journey continues at The Trip, located at 28 NE 14th St #1311, Miami, FL 33132. This intimate indoor space is built for those not ready for the night to end, delivering a deeper after-hours experience with top house and tech-house DJs from around the world alongside Miami’s rising talent. As the energy carries past sunrise, the atmosphere becomes fully immersive and designed for true music lovers. Guests are encouraged to bring positive energy and respect for the space and one another. Sunset to Sunrise combo tickets are available for full access from 9pm to 9am at Astra Rooftop and The Trip for a true all-nighter experience. 21+ only. All ticket sales are final.
